items.headline: Brewers' combined no-hit bid spoiled by Washington's Lile double in 7th inning
items.description:
WASHINGTON (AP) — Washington's Daylen Lile doubled with one out in the seventh to break up a combined no-hitter by Milwaukee's Jacob Misiorowski and Aaron Ashby on Friday night.
Milwaukee left fielder Blake Perkins missed on a diving attempt to catch Lile's double off Ashby.

items.headline: Robinson fined $50,000, Daniels fined $25,000 for roles in Knicks-Hawks altercation
items.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — Mitchell Robinson of the New York Knicks was fined $50,000 and Dyson Daniels of the Atlanta Hawks was fined $25,000, those penalties coming Friday for their roles in an on-court altercation during Game 6 of the teams' Eastern Conference first-round series.
The incident happened with 4:39 left in the second quarter of the Knicks' 140-89 series-clinching win on Thursday night. Both players were assessed technical fouls and ejected.

items.headline: Mariners' Brendan Donovan set for Double-A rehab assignment
items.description:
SEATTLE (AP) — Seattle Mariners third baseman Brendan Donovan will begin a rehab assignment with Double-A Arkansas on Monday, general manager Justin Hollander said.
Donovan left Seattle's 5-0 loss to the Texas Rangers on April 17 with a left hip issue, and then was placed on the 10-day injured list three days later with a left groin muscle strain.

items.headline: Orioles put closer Ryan Helsley on 15-day IL with right elbow inflammation
items.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — Baltimore put closer Ryan Helsley on the 15-day injured list Friday because of right elbow inflammation.
A 31-year-old right-hander signed to a $28 million, two-year contract in December, Helsley is perfect in seven saves chances. He is 0-2 with a 2.53 ERA in 12 appearances, striking out 15 and walking seven in 10 2/3 innings.

items.headline: Texans sign Pro Bowl LB Azeez Al-Shaair to 3-year extension that'll keep him in Houston through 2029
items.description:
HOUSTON (AP) — Pro Bowl linebacker Azeez Al-Shaair will remain with the Houston Texans through the 2029 season after signing a three-year extension this week.
The Texans didn’t release terms of the deal when they announced it Friday, but it is reportedly worth $54 million.

items.headline: Interim coach D.J. Smith will be candidate for LA Kings' full-time job in GM Ken Holland's search
items.description:
LOS ANGELES (AP) — Interim head coach D.J. Smith will be among the candidates for the Los Angeles Kings' full-time job when general manager Ken Holland conducts his coaching search this month.
Smith took over March 1 when Holland fired Jim Hiller with the Kings at 24-21-14 and out of the playoff picture. The former Ottawa head coach rallied Los Angeles to an 11-6-6 finish to claim the last wild-card spot in the Western Conference, but the Kings were swept by the powerhouse Colorado Avalanche in their fifth consecutive first-round postseason exit.

items.headline: Yankees delay Volpe return until at least Monday, with Caballero playing well
items.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — Anthony Volpe is ready to rejoin the New York Yankees, but the team isn't set for the shortstop's return.
With José Caballero playing well, the Yankees kept Volpe at Double-A Somerset on Friday rather than activate him for a series opener against Baltimore.

items.headline: Cink opens 2-shot lead in Regions Tradition in bid for second straight senior major title
items.description:
BIRMINGHAM, Ala. (AP) — Stewart Cink shot his second straight 7-under 65 on Friday to take a two-stroke lead in the Regions Tradition in his bid to won consecutive senior major titles.
Cink won the major Senior PGA Championship two weeks ago in Florida for his third victory of the year on the 50-and-over tour.

items.headline: Raptors' Brandon Ingram out for Game 6 against Cavaliers with sore right heel
items.description:
TORONTO (AP) — Toronto Raptors forward Brandon Ingram was sidelined Friday night for Game 6 of the Eastern Conference first-round playoff series against the Cleveland Cavaliers because of a sore right heel, coach Darko Rajakovic said.
The two-time All-Star left midway through the second quarter of Toronto's 125-120 loss at Cleveland on Wednesday night. He scored one point in 11 minutes before departing.

items.headline: Plane circles Fenway Park for an hour towing banner imploring Red Sox ownership to sell the team
items.description:
BOSTON (AP) — A small plane towing a banner imploring Red Sox ownership to sell the team flew over Fenway Park on Friday while Boston and the Houston Astros took batting practice.
The plane towed a banner with big red letters reading: “FIRE CRAIG! SELL THE TEAM!”
